description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, rounded-corner body finished in a worn beige paint, showing signs of age and surface chipping. The front face features a woven speaker grille on the left, a large circular tuning dial with a transparent cover on the right, and four cylindrical control knobs arranged horizontally along the bottom. The casing appears to be made of wood or Bakelite, with a faux woodgrain trim framing the front panel. The back and sides are plain, with no visible openings or controls, and the rear panel is a solid, dark surface. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, suitable for placement in urban interiors, garages, or retro-themed public spaces.
description: A vintage beige radio receiver with a worn finish, featuring a woven speaker grille, large tuning dial, and four control knobs on the front. The casing is rectangular with rounded edges and a faux woodgrain trim.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the speaker grille, tuning dial, and all four control knobs clearly.
description_view_1: Rear-right view displaying the plain, solid back panel with no visible features or controls.
description_view_2: Rear-left view highlighting the smooth, unadorned back and side surfaces.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the tuning dial and control knobs, with partial visibility of the speaker grille.
